proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with kras_mutation=0 (KRAS wild-type), treatment_cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months than no cetuximab; the cetuximab x kras_mutation interaction term in a linear model of pfs_months will be negative (cetuximab benefit attenuated/abolished in KRAS-mutant patients).
kras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than kras_mutation=0 (poor prognostic effect).

analyses.code: import statsmodels.formula.api as smf; smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_cetuximab * kras_mutation', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab x kras_mutation interaction coefficient = +0.020 (p=0.665). In KRAS WT, cetuximab vs no-cetuximab mean PFS difference = -0.048 months (p=0.124). In KRAS mutant, difference = -0.028 (p=0.376). No evidence cetuximab works preferentially in KRAS WT.
kras_mutation main effect on pfs_months = -0.333 months (p<1e-40); KRAS mutant patients have ~0.33 mo shorter PFS than KRAS WT.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among msi_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with longer pfs_months than no pembrolizumab; the pembrolizumab x msi_high interaction term will be positive (immunotherapy benefit concentrated in MSI-high CRC).
msi_high=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than msi_high=0.

analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab x msi_high interaction = +0.002 (p=0.988). In MSI-high (n=2513), pembro vs no-pembro PFS diff = +0.007 mo (p=0.95); in MSS, diff = +0.005 (p=0.86). No detectable pembrolizumab benefit, no interaction with MSI status.
Mean PFS MSI-high 4.293 mo vs MSS 4.313 mo, diff = -0.019 mo (t-test p=0.682). MSI-high not prognostic in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among braf_v600e=1 patients, treatment_encorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months than no encorafenib; the encorafenib x braf_v600e interaction term will be positive (BRAF inhibitor benefit confined to BRAF V600E-mutant CRC).
braf_v600e=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than braf_v600e=0 (well-established poor prognostic marker in CRC).

analyses.result_summary: Encorafenib x braf_v600e interaction = -0.141 (p=0.376). In BRAF V600E (n=2272), encorafenib vs no PFS diff = -0.129 mo (p=0.36); in BRAF WT, diff = +0.013 (p=0.72). No encorafenib benefit detected, even in V600E subgroup.
Mean PFS BRAF V600E 4.094 vs BRAF WT 4.322, diff = -0.228 mo (p=3.7e-6). BRAF V600E confirmed as poor prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among her2_amplified=1 patients, treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib is associated with longer pfs_months than no T/T; the T/T x her2_amplified interaction will be positive (HER2-targeted therapy benefit confined to HER2-amplified CRC).

analyses.result_summary: Tras/Tuc x her2_amplified interaction = +0.063 (p=0.778). In HER2+ (n=1504), T/T vs no PFS diff = +0.017 mo (p=0.94); main effect of T/T near zero. No evidence of HER2-restricted benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among right_sided_primary=0 (left-sided) patients, treatment_cetuximab is associated with longer pfs_months than no cetuximab; the cetuximab x right_sided_primary interaction will be negative (cetuximab benefit greater in left-sided CRC).
right_sided_primary=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than right_sided_primary=0.

analyses.result_summary: Cetuximab x right_sided interaction = +0.026 (p=0.583). In left-sided, cetuximab diff = -0.049 mo (p=0.088); in right-sided, diff = -0.024 (p=0.49). No cetuximab benefit in either side; sidedness does not modify the (null) cetuximab effect.
right_sided_primary main effect on pfs_months = -0.313 (p<1e-30). Right-sided primaries have ~0.31 mo shorter PFS, consistent with established CRC biology.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib is associated with longer pfs_months than no regorafenib in this cohort, both unadjusted and after adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, cea_ng_ml, and liver_mets.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + cea_ng_ml + liver_mets', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted regorafenib coefficient = +0.939 mo (p<1e-300). Unadjusted mean PFS on-regorafenib 5.09 vs off 4.12 (diff +0.97). Striking, robust prolongation of PFS with regorafenib.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative coefficient).
stage_iv=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive coefficient).
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
liver_mets=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than liver_mets=0.

analyses.result_summary: ecog_ps coef = -1.190 mo per unit (p=0). Strong, dominant prognostic feature.
stage_iv coef = -1.352 mo (p=0). Stage IV cuts PFS by ~1.35 mo on average.
albumin coef = +0.465 mo per g/dL (p=3.2e-115). Higher albumin strongly predicts longer PFS.
cea coef = -0.0053 mo per ng/mL (p=3.1e-17). Higher CEA modestly but significantly shortens PFS.
weight_loss coef = -0.073 mo per percent (p=2.2e-167). Greater weight loss strongly predicts shorter PFS.
liver_mets coef = -0.019 (p=0.52). Surprisingly null in this cohort, contrary to established CRC literature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for ecog_ps and stage_iv, age_years remains independently associated with pfs_months; we expect the direction to be NEGATIVE (older = shorter PFS) but will test the actual sign.

analyses.result_summary: age_years coef = +0.176 mo per year (p=0) unadjusted; +0.176 mo per year (p=0) adjusted for ecog_ps and stage_iv. Direction is POSITIVE (older patients have longer PFS in this dataset), opposite of expected and opposite of typical CRC biology.

proposed_hypotheses.text: nras_mutation=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than nras_mutation=0 (analogous to KRAS).
ntrk_fusion=1 is associated with longer pfs_months than ntrk_fusion=0 (NTRK fusions are usually targetable and rare).

analyses.result_summary: NRAS-mutant (n=1506) mean PFS 4.53 vs WT 4.31, diff = +0.221 mo (p=2.3e-4). Direction is OPPOSITE of expected — NRAS-mutant patients have longer PFS in this cohort.
NTRK fusion (n=251) PFS diff = +0.040 (p=0.78). Null association — and no treatment for NTRK fusions is encoded in the treatment columns.

proposed_hypotheses.text: sex_female=1 is associated with a different mean pfs_months than sex_female=0 (test is two-sided; no strong prior).

analyses.result_summary: Female mean PFS 4.308 vs male 4.314, diff = -0.006 (p=0.77). No sex effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one snp_* column is associated with pfs_months at p<0.05 in a univariate t-test, suggesting some pharmacogenomic signal; family-wise expected by chance ~1.4 / 27 SNPs.

analyses.result_summary: Of 27 SNPs tested, 3 reached p<0.05 (snp_rs1050828 +0.092 mo p=0.015; snp_rs1801131 -0.049 mo p=0.027; snp_rs1801197 -0.049 mo p=0.047). Count is consistent with chance (~1.4 expected); none survive Bonferroni correction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment_X effect on pfs_months differs between stage_iv=1 and stage_iv=0 patients for at least one drug (cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, regorafenib): treatment x stage_iv interaction term will be significant.

analyses.code: for t in treatments: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ {t} * stage_iv', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All four interaction p-values >0.15 (cetux 0.50, bev 0.93, pembro 0.51, regorafenib 0.15). Stage IV does not modify any treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among liver_mets=1 patients, treatment_bevacizumab is associated with longer pfs_months than no bevacizumab; bevacizumab x liver_mets interaction will be positive (anti-VEGF benefit greater with hepatic disease).

analyses.result_summary: Bevacizumab x liver_mets interaction = +0.003 (p=0.97). Main bev effect = -0.020 (p=0.38). No detectable bevacizumab benefit, with or without liver mets.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, albumin_g_dl, cea_ng_ml, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, nlr, kras_mutation, nras_mutation, braf_v600e, msi_high, her2_amplified, and right_sided_primary, only treatment_regorafenib retains a significant adjusted main effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: for t in treatments: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ {t} + <covariates>', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment coefficients (mo): cetux -0.013 p=0.16; bev +0.003 p=0.70; pembro 0.000 p=0.997; encorafenib -0.001 p=0.92; tras/tuc +0.024 p=0.11; regorafenib +0.943 p<1e-300. Only regorafenib has any meaningful adjusted main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, atrial_fibrillation, venous_thromboembolism_history, autoimmune_disease, prior_malignancy, depression_anxiety_diagnosis is associated with shorter pfs_months than its absence (each tested separately).

analyses.result_summary: All 11 comorbidities tested have |diff| < 0.06 mo and p > 0.05 (range 0.05-0.98). No comorbidity is meaningfully associated with PFS in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, and appetite_loss_grade are each associated with shorter pfs_months (negative coefficient).

analyses.code: for s in symptoms: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ {s}', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All five symptom-grade univariate coefficients are tiny and non-significant (cough closest at -0.016, p=0.087). Patient-reported symptom grades carry little prognostic information here.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs by race_ethnicity category (white/black/hispanic/asian/other), specifically with non-white groups expected to have shorter PFS reflecting historical disparities.
Mean pfs_months differs by insurance_type (medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured), with private/medicare expected to have longer PFS.

analyses.result_summary: ANOVA F p=0.479. Group means (mo): asian 4.306, black 4.273, hispanic 4.315, other 4.407, white 4.316. No detectable PFS difference by race/ethnicity (range only 0.13 mo).

ANOVA F p=0.219. Group means (mo): private 4.330, medicare 4.312, medicaid 4.277, uninsured 4.249. Direction is consistent with the hypothesis (uninsured/medicaid lowest), but differences are not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: prior_chemotherapy=1, prior_radiation=1, prior_surgery=1, prior_immunotherapy=1, prior_targeted_therapy=1, and higher prior_lines_of_therapy are each associated with shorter pfs_months (more pretreatment = poorer outcomes).
Higher years_since_diagnosis is associated with longer pfs_months (selection for indolent disease).

analyses.code: for c in prior_tx: scipy.stats.ttest_ind(...); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ prior_lines_of_therapy', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All five prior-therapy binaries non-significant (|diff| <= 0.034 mo, p >= 0.23). prior_lines_of_therapy coef = +0.008 mo per line (p=0.39). No prior-treatment burden effect on PFS.
years_since_diagnosis coef = +0.009 mo/yr (p=0.14). Numerical direction matches hypothesis but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months; higher bmi is associated with longer pfs_months; higher smoking_pack_years is associated with shorter pfs_months; higher education_years is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: for f in [hemoglobin, bmi, smoking_pack_years, education_years]: smf.ols(f'pfs_months ~ {f}', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Univariate coefficients all tiny: hgb -0.010 p=0.078 (wrong direction, marginal); bmi -0.003 p=0.16 (wrong direction); smoking 0.000 p=0.91; education +0.003 p=0.34. None significant; hemoglobin direction opposite of expected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between right_sided_primary and kras_mutation on pfs_months: the joint penalty of being both right-sided AND KRAS-mutant is less than the sum of individual penalties (interaction coefficient > 0).

analyses.result_summary: right_sided coef -0.494; kras coef -0.486; interaction +0.452 (p=2e-25). Strong sub-additive interaction: KRAS-mutant + right-sided patients are not as bad off as adding the two main effects would predict (joint penalty ~-0.53 vs ~-0.98 additive).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib x ecog_ps interaction is non-zero on pfs_months (regorafenib effect varies by performance status).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef +0.005 (p=0.89). Regorafenib's ~+0.95 mo benefit is constant across ECOG levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_regorafenib x kras_mutation interaction on pfs_months is NEGATIVE: regorafenib benefit is attenuated/abolished in KRAS-mutant CRC.
treatment_regorafenib x braf_v600e interaction on pfs_months is NEGATIVE: regorafenib benefit attenuated in BRAF V600E-mutant CRC.
treatment_regorafenib x right_sided_primary interaction on pfs_months is NEGATIVE: regorafenib benefit attenuated in right-sided CRC.
treatment_regorafenib x msi_high interaction on pfs_months is non-zero.

analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib x kras_mutation interaction coef = -1.629 (p=7.7e-226). In KRAS WT, regorafenib effect is +1.66 mo (p<1e-300, n+ 5816); in KRAS mutant, +0.027 mo (p=0.45, n+ 4206). Regorafenib benefit is essentially confined to KRAS WT patients — directionally identical to expected anti-EGFR (cetuximab) biology.

Regorafenib x braf_v600e interaction = -1.183 (p=6.1e-23). In BRAF WT regorafenib effect +1.03 mo (p<1e-300); in BRAF V600E regorafenib effect -0.16 mo (p=0.15). Regorafenib benefit largely absent in BRAF V600E.

Regorafenib x right_sided interaction = -1.454 (p=4e-168). Left-sided regorafenib effect +1.48 mo; right-sided +0.026 mo (p=0.52). Regorafenib's PFS benefit is essentially confined to left-sided primaries.
Regorafenib x msi_high interaction = -0.022 (p=0.85). MSI status does not modify regorafenib effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 'triple-wild-type-and-left-sided' subgroup (kras_mutation=0 AND braf_v600e=0 AND right_sided_primary=0), treatment_regorafenib produces a substantially larger pfs_months gain than the marginal +0.94 mo: estimate >+2 months.

analyses.result_summary: Triple-WT/left-sided n=17372 (3461 on regorafenib, 13911 off). Mean PFS diff = +2.758 mo (p<1e-300). Confirms regorafenib's benefit is concentrated in this enriched subgroup, with effect size ~3x the marginal estimate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable model that includes treatment_regorafenib, kras_mutation, braf_v600e, right_sided_primary AND their pairwise interactions with treatment_regorafenib (plus age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, albumin_g_dl), the main-effect coefficients on kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and right_sided_primary will be reduced toward zero (their apparent prognostic effects are largely mediated by differential regorafenib response, not intrinsic biology).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib + kras_mutation + braf_v600e + right_sided_primary + treatment_regorafenib:kras_mutation + treatment_regorafenib:braf_v600e + treatment_regorafenib:right_sided_primary + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: With regorafenib interactions included: kras_mutation main coef = -0.003 (p=0.74), braf_v600e = 0.000 (p=0.995), right_sided = 0.000 (p=0.96). Regorafenib main effect = +2.276 (p<1e-300); interactions: kras x reg = -1.78, braf x reg = -1.76, right x reg = -1.45 (all p<1e-40). Confirms that the apparent prognostic effects of these biomarkers seen earlier are entirely explained by differential regorafenib response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the five non-regorafenib treatments (cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab_tucatinib), no biomarker-restricted subgroup shows a significant, biologically plausible PFS benefit at p<0.05 after subgroup analysis.
treatment_regorafenib x age_years interaction on pfs_months is non-zero.

analyses.code: Stratified subgroup analyses across 5 non-regorafenib drugs; restated cetuximab in KRAS WT and left-sided, pembrolizumab in MSI-high, encorafenib in BRAF V600E, T/T in HER2+
analyses.result_summary: All canonical biomarker-restricted subgroup analyses for cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, and trastuzumab/tucatinib produce |effect| < 0.13 mo and p>0.08 (most p>0.3). Only regorafenib shows large, biomarker-modulated effects in this dataset.
Regorafenib x age interaction coef = -0.0017 mo/yr (p=0.28). Regorafenib effect is approximately constant across age.
